title: ziziphora hispanica subsp. aragonensis
intro: Ziziphora hispanica subsp. aragonensis, also known as Spanish marjoram, is a subspecies of the Ziziphora plant, a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, Spanish marjoram has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Spanish marjoram can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to relieve stress, promote relaxation, and soothe respiratory issues.
